zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Radcliffe於大約1275年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾拉德克利夫時，她的父親Richard de Radcliffe31歲，母親Margaret le Boteler27歲。她和Henry de Bury至少有2個兒子和4個女兒。她去世於1343年在英國藍卡爾波利享年69歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Radcliffe于大约1275年出生在英国英格兰藍卡爾拉德克利夫时，她的父亲Richard de Radcliffe31岁，母亲Margaret le Boteler27岁。她和Henry de Bury至少有2个儿子和4个女儿。她去世于1343年在英国藍卡爾波利享年69岁。

nameMeanings.name: Radcliffe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Ratliff
meanings: English (Suffolk): habitational name from one or more of the places so named, such as Radcliffe (Lancashire), Radcliffe on Trent and Ratcliffe on Soar (both Nottinghamshire), Radclive (Buckinghamshire), Ratcliffe Culey and Ratcliffe on the Wreake (both Leicestershire), Ratclyffe in Clyst Hydon, Ratcliffes in Thorverton, and Ratcliffes in Broad Clyst (all Devon), Ratcliff in Stepney (Middlesex), and Rackley in Compton Bishop (Somerset). The placenames derive from Old English rēad ‘red’ + clif ‘cliff, bank, steep slope’ (see also Rutley ). A family of the name Radcliffe trace their descent from Sir Nicholas de Radclyffe. He is said to have been a knight who held the major of Radcliffe in Lancashire and served Roger de Poitou, Baron de Marsey in the 11th century.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Radcliffe was born about 1275, in Radcliffe, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Richard de Radcliffe, was 31 and her mother, Margaret le Boteler, was 27. She had at least 2 sons and 4 daughters with Henry de Bury. She died in 1343, in Bury, Lancashire, England, at the age of 69.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Radcliffe est née environ 1275, à Radcliffe, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard de Radcliffe, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Margaret le Boteler, avait 27 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles avec Henry de Bury. Elle est décédée en 1343, à Bury, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 69 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Radcliffe um 1275 in Radcliffe, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard de Radcliffe 31 und ihre Mutter Margaret le Boteler 27. Sie hatte mit Henry de Bury mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb 1343 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Bury, Lancashire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Radcliffe è nata il circa 1275 nel Radcliffe, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Richard de Radcliffe, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Margaret le Boteler, aveva 27 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie con Henry de Bury. Morì nel 1343, nel Bury, Lancashire, England, all'età di 69 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Radcliffe(이)가 대략 1275년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 라드클리프에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard de Radcliffe(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret le Boteler(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 Henry de Bury(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1343년 영국 랭카실르 버이에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери от Henry de Bury. Она умерла в 1343 г. в местности бури, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 69 лет.
